candidate_summary: PENILLION-COUNTER: 2-agent fixed-vs-improvised counterpoint where Agent A runs a fixed deterministic policy, Agent B emits a non-redundant counter-melody constrained by KL(B||A) >= epsilon, and aggregator Agent C produces a harmonic-resolution output via weighted ensembling. Inspired by Welsh penillion cerdd dant.

results_analysis.title: On the Design of KL-Regularized Policy Gradient Algorithms for LLM Reasoning (2025/05)
kw_matches: KL-divergence regularizer (partial: stay-close direction vs non-redundancy)
results_analysis.reason: sem>=0.7 AND func>=0.7: KL-regularized policy gradient is structurally the same mathematical machinery as the proposed KL non-redundancy regularizer; direction differs but apparatus is identical.

results_analysis.title: Residual Policy Gradient: A Reward View of KL-regularized Objective (2025/03)
results_analysis.reason: sem>=0.7 AND func>=0.7: reference + residual policy is an exact structural twin of fixed harp + penillion counterpoint; only direction of residual differs.

results_analysis.title: Beyond KL Divergence: GBMPO Bregman regularizer (Lexsi)
results_analysis.reason: sem>=0.7: discusses Bregman/KL regularizer family which is the same generic regularizer apparatus as PENILLION's KL constraint.

results_analysis.title: From Debate to Equilibrium: Belief-Driven Multi-Agent LLM Reasoning Bayesian Nash (OpenReview)
kw_matches: hierarchical aggregator (partial match to third-aggregator harmonic resolution)
results_analysis.reason: sem>=0.7 AND func>=0.7: ECON hierarchical-RL Bayesian Nash equilibrium with aggregator is functional twin of harmonic-resolution aggregator over multi-agent outputs.

results_analysis.title: Multiagent Debate for Factuality and Reasoning (ICML 2024)
kw_matches: aggregator/majority voting matches third-aggregator harmonic resolution
results_analysis.reason: sem>=0.7 AND func>=0.7: canonical multi-agent debate + aggregator is the direct prior art that PENILLION recombines, making it an asymmetric MAD variant.

results_analysis.title: Adaptive Heterogeneous Multi-Agent Debate (Springer 2025)
results_analysis.reason: sem>=0.7 AND func>=0.7: heterogeneous role-asymmetric MAD covers the fixed-vs-improvised asymmetry distinguishing claim.

results_analysis.title: Improving Factuality through Multiagent Debate (Du 2023)
results_analysis.reason: sem>=0.7 AND func>=0.7: foundational MAD with aggregator; canonical prior art for the multi-agent + aggregator pattern.

results_analysis.title: Multi-Agent Debate Adaptive Stability Detection (Vusd1Hw2D9 OpenReview)
results_analysis.reason: sem>=0.7 AND func>=0.7: adaptive stability detection + consensus optimizer weights cover the aggregator-with-weighted-ensembling component.

verdict_sentence: PENILLION-COUNTER FAILS verification: 8/8 results hit (8 semantic, 7 functional) — KL-regularized PG, residual policy gradient, MAD with aggregators, and heterogeneous role-asymmetric MAD jointly cover the proposed fixed-policy + KL-constrained improviser + aggregator design.
